title: Weird 'stuck' problem with cocotb using verilator versions after 4.106
body:
Hi again, with another cocotb/verilator story :)

I have now switched to an MacBook Air M1 and now I have a weird issue with my codebase where verilator does not seem to answer cocotb anymore, at least that is how it seems

versions are in the log below, but it is the same behaviour for verilator 108 as well.

```
0.00ns INFO     Running on Verilator version 4.111 devel
     0.00ns INFO     Running tests with cocotb v1.5.0.dev307+g9fe131d9 from /opt/homebrew/Cellar/python@3.9/3.9.2_1/Frameworks/Python.framework/Versions/3.9/lib/python3.9/site-packages/cocotb
     0.00ns INFO     Seeding Python random module with 1614331972
     0.00ns INFO     Found test svb_clk_div_tb.test_clk_div
     0.00ns INFO     Found test svb_clk_div_tb.test_clk_div_param_assert
     0.00ns INFO     Running test 1/2: test_clk_div
     0.00ns DEBUG    Trigger fired: <NullTrigger for Start <Test test_clk_div> at 0x106230ec0>
     0.00ns DEBUG    1 pending coroutines for event <NullTrigger for Start <Test test_clk_div> at 0x106230ec0>
                     	test_clk_div
     0.00ns DEBUG    Scheduling coroutine test_clk_div
     0.00ns DEBUG    Scheduling with Value(None)
     0.00ns INFO     Starting test: "test_clk_div"
                     Description:  Test that clock is divided according to param DIV_BY.
     0.00ns DEBUG    Adding new coroutine Clock.start
     0.00ns DEBUG    Scheduling with Value(None)
     0.00ns DEBUG    Adding new coroutine Scheduler._do_writes
     0.00ns DEBUG    Scheduling with Value(None)
     0.00ns DEBUG    Coroutine Scheduler._do_writes yielded <<Event at 0x1054a30a0>.wait() at 0x105c82d40> (mode 1)
     0.00ns DEBUG    Coroutine Clock.start yielded <Timer of 5000.00ps at 0x105db5380> (mode 1)
     0.00ns INFO     Reset dividers.
     0.00ns DEBUG    Coroutine test_clk_div yielded FallingEdge(ModifiableObject(svb_clk_div_tb.clk_i)) (mode 1)
     0.00ns DEBUG    Scheduled coroutine test_clk_div
     0.00ns DEBUG    1 pending coroutines for event <<Event at 0x1054a30a0>.wait() at 0x105c82d40>
                     	Scheduler._do_writes
     0.00ns DEBUG    Scheduling coroutine Scheduler._do_writes
     0.00ns DEBUG    Scheduling with Value(<<Event at 0x1054a30a0>.wait() at 0x105c82d40>)
     0.00ns DEBUG    Coroutine Scheduler._do_writes yielded ReadWrite() (mode 1)
     0.00ns DEBUG    Scheduled coroutine Scheduler._do_writes
     0.00ns DEBUG    All coroutines scheduled, handing control back to simulator
     0.00ns DEBUG    Trigger fired: ReadWrite()
     0.00ns DEBUG    1 pending coroutines for event ReadWrite()
                     	Scheduler._do_writes
     0.00ns DEBUG    Scheduling coroutine Scheduler._do_writes
     0.00ns DEBUG    Scheduling with Value(ReadWrite())
     0.00ns DEBUG    Coroutine Scheduler._do_writes yielded <<Event at 0x1054a30a0>.wait() at 0x106230880> (mode 1)
     0.00ns DEBUG    Scheduled coroutine Scheduler._do_writes
     0.00ns DEBUG    All coroutines scheduled, handing control back to simulator
```

it gets completely stuck after the last handover form cocotb. I cannot even ctrl+c it, I must send a TERM signal from another shell.

Is there any way to debug this further on verilators end? From the log it does not look like a cocotb problem, adding @ktbarrett anyway for notification.

I have not observed this on my Intel macMini nor in my Linux or WSL environments.

It could be totally unrelated to the arm architecture, but it currently somewhat feels like it.
